steps.title: Identify the appositive relationship
steps.content: The phrase needs to connect “fellow OECD member nation” to “Costa Rica.” Costa Rica is essential to specify which member nation is being discussed (there are 38 OECD members), making it a restrictive appositive—one that identifies or clarifies the noun it follows, not one providing optional extra information.

steps.title: Apply the restrictive appositive rule
steps.content: Restrictive appositives do not require punctuation. C is correct because “nation Costa Rica” follows this convention with no punctuation between the common noun and the proper noun that identifies it. Choice A uses a colon, which doesn't introduce a single proper noun in Standard English; choice B adds an em dash that would be unnecessary for a restrictive appositive; choice D adds a comma, which marks non-restrictive appositives (where the information is optional). That matches choice C—C is correct.
